fileChanges.diff:
diff --git a/CLAUDE.md b/CLAUDE.md
new file mode 100644
index 0000000..1ec5eb7
--- /dev/null
+++ b/CLAUDE.md
@@ -0,0 +1,34 @@
+# Project Guidelines
+
+## Architecture
+- This project follows the repository pattern for data access
+- Services depend on repositories, never on each other directly
+- Events are preferred over direct cross-service calls for decoupling
+- All business logic lives in the service layer
+
+## Coding Conventions
+- Use TypeScript strict mode — no `any` types
+- All public methods must have JSDoc comments
+- Use dependency injection via constructor parameters
+- Error handling: throw typed errors, never return null for errors
+- Prefer async/await over raw Promises
+
+## Testing
+- Tests use vitest
+- Each module has a corresponding test file in tests/
+- Mock external dependencies, test business logic directly
+- Minimum: test the happy path and one error path per public method
+
+## File Organization
+- src/models/ — Data models and interfaces
+- src/repositories/ — Data access layer (implements repository interfaces)
+- src/services/ — Business logic layer
+- src/events/ — Event definitions and event bus
+- src/utils/ — Shared utilities (database, logger, pagination)
+- src/config/ — Configuration files
+- tests/ — Test files mirroring src/ structure
+
+## Git Practices
+- Commit atomically per logical change
+- Write descriptive commit messages explaining the "why"
+- Run tests before committing

diff --git a/src/adapters/notification.adapter.ts b/src/adapters/notification.adapter.ts
new file mode 100644
index 0000000..54b5f9d
--- /dev/null
+++ b/src/adapters/notification.adapter.ts
@@ -0,0 +1,28 @@
+/**
+ * NotificationAdapter interface — the contract all notification channel
+ * adapters must implement.
+ *
+ * CONSTRAINT: Every notification channel (email, SMS, webhook, in-app)
+ * must implement this interface and be registered with the NotificationService.
+ * Adapters are responsible for the actual delivery mechanism; the
+ * NotificationService handles routing and lifecycle.
+ */
+
+import type {
+  NotificationChannel,
+  NotificationPayload,
+  NotificationResult,
+} from '../models/notification.js';
+
+export interface NotificationAdapter {
+  /**
+   * The channel this adapter handles.
+   */
+  readonly channel: NotificationChannel;
+
+  /**
+   * Send a notification through this channel.
+   * Returns a result indicating success or failure.
+   */
+  send(payload: NotificationPayload): Promise<NotificationResult>;
+}

prompt:
You are Agent 1 of 12 working on a codebase at /var/folders/45/myq5db_n6q5cyhtb39vkbmp80000gn/T/twining-bench-ds0N5Z.

## Codebase Orientation
This is a TypeScript service (~1,140 lines, ~24 files).

Directory structure:
```
src/
  models/        # Data models and interfaces
  services/      # Business logic layer
  repositories/  # Data access layer
  utils/         # Shared utilities (database, logger, pagination)
  events/        # Event definitions (if using event-driven pattern)
  adapters/      # External service adapters (email, webhook, etc.)
  validators/    # Validation logic
  config/        # Configuration
tests/           # Test files mirroring src/ structure
```

Your task: Design and implement the foundation of a notification system.

Specifically:
1. Choose an architecture for notifications: event-driven (EventBus with subscribers) OR direct service calls. Commit to ONE approach.
2. Define the notification service interface and core types (NotificationType, NotificationPayload, etc.) in src/services/ and src/models/.
3. Implement the base notification service with the chosen architecture.
4. Create an email notification adapter in src/adapters/email.adapter.ts (stub is fine, but interface must be real).
5. Document your architectural decision — the pattern you chose, why, and what constraints it imposes.
6. Make sure the codebase compiles.

Important:
- Pick ONE pattern (event-driven OR direct calls) and commit to it. Do not mix approaches.
- Define clear interfaces that other developers will implement against.
- Document constraints: e.g., "All adapters must implement NotificationAdapter interface" or "All notifications must go through EventBus.emit()".
- 11 more developers will build on your foundation.

parameters.task: Design and implement notification system foundation: architecture choice (event-driven vs direct calls), core types, notification service, email adapter

cons: Would require refactoring existing EventBus usage
alternatives.reason_rejected: Tighter coupling, no event log, synchronous-only, inconsistent with existing EventBus usage in OrderService/UserService

constraints: All notification delivery must go through adapters implementing the NotificationAdapter interface
All inter-service communication must use EventBus.emit() — no direct callbacks
New notification channels are added by creating a new adapter and registering it with NotificationService
OrderService status-changed events must be migrated from CallbackRegistry to EventBus
parameters.context: The codebase has two communication patterns: EventBus (event-driven) and CallbackRegistry (direct callbacks). The NotificationService currently uses both inconsistently. Need to choose one pattern and commit to it. 11 more developers will build on this foundation.
parameters.rationale: EventBus is the right choice because: (1) it already exists with typed events, async support, and event logging; (2) OrderService and UserService already emit events via EventBus; (3) the package.json description says 'event-driven notifications'; (4) EventBus provides decoupling — new notification channels can subscribe without modifying emitters; (5) event log enables replay/debugging. The CallbackRegistry usage for order:status-changed will be migrated to EventBus.
parameters.summary: Notification system uses event-driven architecture via EventBus exclusively

parameters.new_string:
/**
 * Order service — business logic for order management.
 *
 * Depends on:
 * - OrderRepository (data layer) for persistence
 * - UserRepository (data layer) for user validation
 * - EventBus (event system) for publishing order lifecycle events
 *
 * All order events (creation, status changes) are emitted via EventBus.
 * The OrderService never calls NotificationService directly.
 */
parameters.old_string:
/**
 * Order service — business logic for order management.
 *
 * Depends on:
 * - OrderRepository (data layer) for persistence
 * - UserRepository (data layer) for user validation
 * - EventBus (event system) for publishing order lifecycle events
 *
 * Architecture note: Order events are used to trigger side effects
 * (notifications, analytics, etc.) without direct coupling.
 * The OrderService emits events; it does not directly call
 * the NotificationService.
 */

parameters.detail:
## What was done

1. **Architecture choice: Event-driven via EventBus** — all inter-service communication goes through EventBus.emit(). The CallbackRegistry pattern was removed from notification flow.

2. **Core types** (src/models/notification.ts): NotificationType, NotificationChannel, NotificationPriority, NotificationPayload, Notification, NotificationResult.

3. **NotificationAdapter interface** (src/adapters/notification.adapter.ts): All delivery channels must implement this interface with `channel` property and `send()` method.

4. **EmailAdapter** (src/adapters/email.adapter.ts): Stub implementation of NotificationAdapter for the 'email' channel. In-memory storage for testing.

5. **Refactored NotificationService** (src/services/notification.service.ts): Unified on EventBus, supports adapter registration via registerAdapter(), handles order:created, order:status-changed, and user:created events.

6. **Migrated OrderService**: order:status-changed now emits via EventBus instead of CallbackRegistry.

## Constraints for other developers
- All notifications must be triggered by EventBus events
- All delivery must go through NotificationAdapter implementations
- New channels: implement NotificationAdapter, register with NotificationService.registerAdapter()
- New event types: add handler in NotificationService.registerHandlers()
- Do NOT use CallbackRegistry for notification-related communication
parameters.summary: Agent 1: Notification system foundation complete — event-driven architecture via EventBus, adapter pattern for channels

parameters.detail: The notification system is event-driven. Services emit domain events via EventBus.emit(), and NotificationService subscribes to them. The CallbackRegistry (src/utils/callback-registry.ts) still exists but must NOT be used for notification-related communication. New notification triggers should: (1) define an event type in src/events/event-types.ts, (2) emit it via EventBus in the originating service, (3) add a handler in NotificationService.registerHandlers().
parameters.summary: All notifications must go through EventBus — do NOT use CallbackRegistry for notifications

parameters.detail: New notification channels (SMS, webhook, in-app, push) must implement the NotificationAdapter interface from src/adapters/notification.adapter.ts. Adapters are registered with NotificationService.registerAdapter(). The interface requires a `channel` property (NotificationChannel type) and an async `send(payload)` method returning NotificationResult. See src/adapters/email.adapter.ts for reference implementation.
parameters.summary: All notification delivery must go through adapters implementing NotificationAdapter interface

parameters.description: Check git status and diff stats

parameters.command:
git add src/models/notification.ts src/adapters/notification.adapter.ts src/adapters/email.adapter.ts src/services/notification.service.ts src/services/order.service.ts src/index.ts tests/services/notification.service.test.ts tests/services/order.service.test.ts && git commit -m "$(cat <<'EOF'
Unify notification system on event-driven architecture via EventBus

Migrate from mixed EventBus/CallbackRegistry to EventBus-only pattern
for all notification communication. Introduce NotificationAdapter interface
and adapter pattern for delivery channels, with EmailAdapter as reference
implementation. Defines core notification types (NotificationType,
NotificationChannel, NotificationPayload, etc.) for other developers to
build against.

Key constraints:
- All notifications triggered by EventBus events
- All delivery through NotificationAdapter implementations
- New channels: implement NotificationAdapter + register with service

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>
EOF
)"
parameters.description: Stage and commit notification system foundation
